summary: The test-debugger-exec test timed out after 15 seconds while waiting for the debugger to emit a pattern matching /break (?:on start )?in/i. Although the debugger attached successfully, the expected break message never appeared within the timeout window.
root_cause: Test execution timeout: waitFor() in test/common/debugger.js:92 did not receive the expected pattern /break (?:on start )?in/i from the debugger within the 15000 ms timeout, causing the test parallel/test-debugger-exec to fail.

evidence.quote: Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i
evidence.reason: Explicit error message showing the test exceeded its 15-second timeout while waiting for a specific pattern from the debugger
Stack trace indicates the timeout occurred in waitForInitialBreak, confirming the debugger never produced the expected initial break message
Confirms this specific test was the only failure among 5175 tests run, isolating the issue to debugger test execution
Shows the debugger did initialize and attach, but the expected break pattern was never emitted in the captured output

suggested_fix: Investigate why the debugger on macOS CI is not emitting the expected break pattern, or increase the timeout in test/common/debugger.js:92 if the pattern is simply slow to appear on this system. Check for debugger output format changes or performance regressions.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
